points.text: Proxygen appointed Chiara Conti, Ph.D. as Chief Scientific Officer to lead clinical translation of its molecular glue degrader platform and expand into broader induced proximity therapeutics.

Erasca announced preliminary Phase 1 dose escalation data for ERAS-0015, a pan-RAS molecular glue, showing confirmed and unconfirmed partial responses in RAS-mutant solid tumors at doses as low as 8 mg once daily.
Proxygen maintains a dense pipeline of molecular glue degraders and has established multiple pharmaceutical partnerships, including with Merck & Co, to advance proximity-based therapeutics.

Erasca announced positive preliminary Phase 1 dose escalation data for ERAS-0015, a pan-RAS molecular glue, showing 62% unconfirmed objective response rate (uORR) in KRAS G12X NSCLC 2L+ patients at 16-32 mg QD PAD.
Erasca's ERAS-0015 demonstrated 40% uORR in 2L KRAS G12X PDAC at 16-32 mg QD PAD and 50% uORR at 32 mg QD in the ongoing AURORAS-1 Phase 1 trial.

Preliminary data for ERAS-0015 indicate a favorable safety profile with no dose-limiting toxicities (DLTs) observed as of the data cutoff and low rates of dose reductions or discontinuations due to treatment-related adverse events.
Erasca plans to narrow data disclosure for select ERAS-0015 monotherapy dose expansion and combination dose escalation cohorts to the first half of 2027.

Erasca — ERAS-0015 — RAS-mutant solid tumors — Phase 1 AURORAS-1 trial: confirmed and unconfirmed partial responses observed at doses ≥8 mg QD; favorable safety, linear PK, and potential best-in-class profile.

Proxygen — Molecular glue degrader platform — Induced proximity therapeutics — Appointed Chiara Conti as CSO to transition from discovery to clinical-stage execution; expanding beyond degradation to new effector classes.
Erasca — ERAS-0015 — KRAS-mutant solid tumors — Phase 1 AURORAS-1 trial reported 62% uORR in 2L+ KRAS G12X NSCLC and 40% uORR in 2L KRAS G12X PDAC at 16-32 mg QD PAD.
Erasca — ERAS-0015 — KRAS-mutant solid tumors — Preliminary combination data with panitumumab showed no DLTs in N=3 patients and 1/1 unconfirmed partial response in CRC.
Proxygen — Clinical-stage expansion of molecular glue platform — Next-generation induced proximity therapeutics — Appointed Chiara Conti (ex-Blueprint Medicines) as CSO; partnered with Merck & Co.
Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Phase 1 AURORAS-1 trial ongoing; demonstrating partial responses in RAS-mutant solid tumors with favorable ADME/PK properties.
Erasca — Pan-RAS molecular glue development — ERAS-0015 — Phase 1 dose escalation completed with robust efficacy in NSCLC and PDAC; combination cohorts with panitumumab ongoing.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue inhibiting RAS signaling and preventing resistance via wildtype variant inhibition — Phase: 1 (AURORAS-1) — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors — Comparator: None stated — Results: Confirmed/unconfirmed partial responses at ≥8 mg QD; linear PK; favorable safety.

Proxygen pipeline — Mechanism: Molecular glue degraders and induced proximity effectors — Phase: Discovery to IND-enabling — Population: High-value targets beyond current scope — Results: Multiple development candidates identified; platform expanded beyond degradation.

ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: 1 (AURORAS-1) — Population: KRAS-mutant solid tumors (NSCLC, PDAC, CRC) — Comparator: None (monotherapy data) — Endpoints/Results: 62% uORR in 2L+ KRAS G12X NSCLC; 40% uORR in 2L KRAS G12X PDAC; no DLTs observed.
ERAS-0015 — Mechanism: Pan-RAS molecular glue — Phase: 1 — Population: RAS-mutant solid tumors — PK: Dose-dependent exposure up to 40 mg QD with no plateau observed.
Proxygen — Strategic partnership expansion — Molecular glue degraders — Established multiple partnerships including with Merck & Co (MSD) to advance proximity-based therapeutics.
